manifest.title: U.S.S. Glass Harbor: Drowned Constellation - Open World

ship.name: U.S.S. Glass Harbor
ship.openingCondition: Recently assigned to a ninety-day Nerine Reef route-security and survey deployment. The ship is serviceable and well suited to escort, sensing, towing, and probe work, but its replacement gravimetric pallet has not been fully characterized under Reef shear.

commandStructure.playerRole: Principal mission commander at campaign start and Acting Captain after the opening gravitic inversion.

serviceHistory.commissioning: Entered service during the 2360s as a medium-range tactical support and patrol vessel.
serviceHistory.wartimeService: Convoy escort, frontier patrol, rapid reinforcement, debris-field clearance, and route survey during the early Dominion conflict.
serviceHistory.currentAssignment: Nerine Reef route survey, civilian rescue support, freedom-of-navigation patrol, and frontier stabilization.
openProductionDecisions: Detailed prior captain history before Amina Rhos

missionProfile: Survey unstable gravitic routes and maintain a reliable regional sounding picture.
Escort civilian traffic through dangerous corridors without becoming a permanent occupation force.
Conduct rescue, salvage-safety, and emergency-towing operations.
Investigate the cause of route instability and the disappearance of Captain Rhos.
Support local diplomatic arrangements concerning charts, passage, sanctuary, and security.

keyCapabilities: Long-range gravimetric and subspace sensor suite
Medium cruiser warp and impulse endurance
Strong tractor and structural-support capability for its size
Thirty-two dedicated gravitic survey buoys at campaign start
Two shuttlecraft and four workbees configured for route sounding and rescue
Tactical systems adequate for escort and raider deterrence but not regional dominance
knownTechnicalDebt: Replacement gravimetric sensor pallet drifts during rapid shear transitions
Sustained high-load tractor operations overheat port-side power trunks
Buoy stores are finite; recovery, repair, and redeployment matter
Reef dust causes accelerated wear in shuttle impulse manifolds and exterior sensor apertures
constraints: The ship cannot hold every route, rescue every settlement, and confront every armed faction simultaneously.
High-energy scans can perturb local gravitic geometry and reveal concealed traffic.
The Steamrunner-class tactical profile makes some local actors assume Starfleet intends enforcement even when the mission is scientific.
The ship has no large civilian evacuation capacity and must coordinate local hulls for mass movement.

startingStores.medicalReliefCapacity: limited but expandable through local partnerships
startingStores.shuttleAvailability: two available at opening; one is lost with Rhos during the prelude unless recovered later

ship.playerSafeSummary: A recognizable medium Starfleet vessel built for escort, patrol, survey, and rapid-response work. It is capable enough to alter regional outcomes but too small to impose a simple solution on the Nerine Reef.

seniorStaff.name: Amina Rhos
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: Patient, exacting, and known for publishing complete survey records. Her disappearance creates the opening succession.

seniorStaff.name: Ishan Tal
seniorStaff.billet: Chief Science Officer and Gravitic Cartographer
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: A precise, fast-speaking cartographer who believes incomplete charts kill people.

seniorStaff.name: Mara Venn
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: A blunt frontier engineer who knows the Glass Harbor can exceed regulations only by accumulating debt.

seniorStaff.name: Shara zh’Thenn
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: A disciplined tactical officer who sees uncontrolled navigation data as a military threat.

seniorStaff.name: Olan Rix
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: A calm and unsentimental physician who challenges plans that externalize danger onto displaced people.

seniorStaff.name: Sorren Vale
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: A socially observant operations officer attentive to informal systems, crew division, and local fear.

seniorStaff.name: T’Kessa
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: A trained gravitic pilot who believes she should have accompanied Rhos on the missing shuttle.

seniorStaff.name: Hark
seniorStaff.playerSafeSummary: The deck chief overseeing probes, shuttles, recovery teams, and the practical limits of every elegant plan.

supportingCrew.name: Ensign Eshren Kor
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: Accompanies Rhos on the beacon shuttle and survives at Kappa-4 unless the survival clock reaches a lethal threshold.

supportingCrew.name: Lieutenant Leena Park
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: A systems integration specialist suitable for delegated repair and buoy-fabrication assignments.

supportingCrew.name: Lieutenant junior grade Thalen Shaar
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: A disciplined away-team leader who can carry independent security authority when zh’Thenn remains aboard.

supportingCrew.name: Ensign Niri Zhiv

campaignContext.eraLabel: 2373, during the Dominion War and while Voyager remains in the Delta Quadrant
campaignContext.missionProfile: Frontier survey, route-security patrol, civilian rescue, escort, salvage safety, and regional diplomacy in the Nerine Reef.
campaignContext.playerRoleRule: Locked role: newly promoted Commander and Executive Officer. The player becomes Acting Captain after Captain Amina Rhos disappears in the opening mission.

lockedRole.roleLabel: Newly promoted XO and prospective Acting Captain
lockedRole.commandAuthority: Principal mission commander at opening; full operational command as Acting Captain after the prelude.
lockedRole.captainAuthorityBoundary: Rhos retains legal command while present. Her disappearance creates a lawful succession rather than an informal power grab; if she returns, command status is resolved through play and Starfleet review rather than automatically removing player agency.

ageBands.label: Young for the role
ageBands.summary: A comparatively new Commander whose promotion is credible but immediately tested.

ageBands.label: Established command-track officer
ageBands.summary: An officer with enough department and field experience to be a conventional XO selection.

ageBands.label: Late transition into command
ageBands.summary: A seasoned specialist or staff officer taking a major line-command billet later than usual.

allowedSpecies.summary: Must fit 24th-century Starfleet service and may not begin with campaign-secret knowledge.

careerBackgrounds.label: Flight operations and navigation
careerBackgrounds.summary: Piloting, route planning, shuttle command, and practical risk judgment.

careerBackgrounds.label: Science and cartography
careerBackgrounds.summary: Survey practice, evidence custody, disciplined uncertainty, and field research.

careerBackgrounds.label: Engineering and rescue operations
careerBackgrounds.summary: Damage control, towing, systems triage, and technical accountability.

careerBackgrounds.label: Security and escort command
careerBackgrounds.summary: Rules of engagement, convoy protection, boarding operations, and threat assessment.

careerBackgrounds.label: Medical and humanitarian operations
careerBackgrounds.summary: Mass-casualty response, evacuation, quarantine, and civilian coordination.

careerBackgrounds.label: Diplomatic and border service
careerBackgrounds.summary: Jurisdiction, mixed authorities, local custom, and de-escalation.

careerBackgrounds.label: Intelligence and analysis
careerBackgrounds.summary: Pattern recognition, source validation, counter-deception, and compartmented information.

careerBackgrounds.label: Line officer generalist
careerBackgrounds.summary: A broad command-track record without one dominant specialty.

formativeExperiences.label: Dominion War convoy duty
formativeExperiences.summary: Wartime operations where routing and logistics mattered as much as combat.

formativeExperiences.label: A lost survey or away team
formativeExperiences.summary: An operation where incomplete data and delayed command judgment carried lasting consequences.

formativeExperiences.label: Frontier autonomy dispute
formativeExperiences.summary: Service where formal Federation authority and legitimate local authority diverged.

formativeExperiences.label: Disaster evacuation
formativeExperiences.summary: A crisis that required prioritization, civilian coordination, and acceptance of imperfect outcomes.

formativeExperiences.label: Compartmented mission
formativeExperiences.summary: Experience with necessary secrecy and the institutional costs it can create.

formativeExperiences.label: First-contact or cultural liaison duty
formativeExperiences.summary: Work where language, trust, and assumptions about knowledge were operational factors.

formativeExperiences.label: Starbase or fleet staff service
formativeExperiences.summary: Planning, readiness, law, and the distance between administrative systems and field reality.

formativeExperiences.label: Routine professional service
formativeExperiences.summary: A credible career without one defining catastrophe, leaving the campaign to become the officer’s major test.

assignmentReasons.label: Requested by Captain Rhos
assignmentReasons.summary: Rhos selected the officer for judgment that complements her own survey doctrine.

assignmentReasons.label: Promoted into an urgent vacancy
assignmentReasons.summary: The officer was advanced because Starfleet needed a credible XO immediately.

assignmentReasons.label: Relevant specialist experience
assignmentReasons.summary: The assignment reflects unusual expertise in navigation, rescue, diplomacy, or frontier systems.

assignmentReasons.label: Wartime transfer
assignmentReasons.summary: The posting resulted from fleet-wide movement of experienced officers during the Dominion conflict.

assignmentReasons.label: Deliberate command test
assignmentReasons.summary: Starfleet intends the billet to determine whether the officer is ready for permanent command.

options.label: Overcontrol
options.summary: The officer has difficulty delegating when consequences are uncertain.

options.label: Needs certainty to project confidence
options.summary: Ambiguous evidence can delay judgment or produce excessive qualification.

options.label: Protective secrecy
options.summary: The officer may withhold information to prevent panic or misuse.

options.label: Institutional reflex
options.summary: The officer initially trusts formal procedure over local legitimacy.

options.label: Personalizes failure
options.summary: Loss or error can become a private obligation that distorts future risk.

options.label: Impatient with politics
options.summary: The officer prefers operational clarity even when political process is itself part of the solution.

world.title: The Nerine Reef
world.summary: A gravitic frontier maze on the Federation-Kheled boundary, inhabited by hidden communities, salvage families, patrol stations, raiders, and people whose survival depends on route knowledge that can never be politically neutral.

locations.title: Lagrange Gate
locations.playerSummary: The Federation-side entrance to the Nerine Reef, centered on a damaged navigation platform and a congregation of vessels waiting for escort.
locations.directorSummary: Lagrange Gate is the safest communications point and the easiest location for outside authorities to project power. Crowding, inspection delays, and route scarcity turn routine traffic control into political pressure.
locations.everydayLife: Courier crews barter current soundings in station corridors; families sleep aboard ships waiting for safe passage; repair teams work continuously on the navigation platform.

locations.title: Kheled Watch
locations.playerSummary: A compact Kheled patrol station controlling the Protectorate’s most reliable approach to the Reef.
locations.directorSummary: Kheled Watch can supply repair stores, military pilots, and disciplined patrol support, but it treats navigation access as a security concession. Prefect Tal Oren is pragmatic; Subprefect Jorad Pel is actively manufacturing evidence for intervention.
locations.everydayLife: The station is austere and heavily scheduled. Registered merchants receive predictable service; unregistered habitats and improvised craft experience delays and scrutiny.

locations.title: Quiet Shelf
locations.playerSummary: A relatively stable basin containing a listening array, an agricultural habitat, and enough clear space for sensor calibration and flight training.
locations.directorSummary: Quiet Shelf supports calm content, repairs, crew conversations, and calibration. Time spent here is safe but allows other fronts to advance. It is not secretly a villain base.
locations.everydayLife: The agricultural habitat runs on communal shifts and treats visiting crews to fresh produce. The listening array’s technicians keep meticulous handwritten route weather logs.

locations.title: Aster Basin
locations.playerSummary: The largest civilian concentration in the Reef and the informal center of the Drift Concord, assembled from habitats, converted freighters, and tethered platforms around a dim brown dwarf.
locations.directorSummary: Aster Basin survives through concealment, shared pilots, and mutual aid. Recording or transmitting its exact coordinates changes Chart Exposure. The basin can become a trusted ally, a resentful neutral, an occupied dependency, or a mass-casualty site.
locations.everydayLife: Markets move between docked hulls, classrooms rotate through ships with available pressure, and public meetings are held by narrowband relay so no single habitat becomes a permanent capital.

locations.title: Saint Caligo Wreckfield
locations.playerSummary: Hundreds of wrecks from several conflicts orbit a compact gravitic knot, many stripped, some armed, and a few still sealed.
locations.directorSummary: Saint Caligo supports repeated visits. It contains survivor recoveries, cultural claims, weapons hazards, Shepherd components, and evidence from the Kheled-Crown border crisis. No single expedition exhausts the location.
locations.everydayLife: Breakwater divers mark safe hulls with amber lamps; memorial transmitters recite names on old emergency bands; salvage families live aboard tugs that rarely leave the field.

locations.title: Crown Shoal
locations.playerSummary: A maze of dense fragments and sensor shadows intermittently controlled by the Crown of Embers.
locations.directorSummary: Crown Shoal contains illicit docks, hostage exchanges, black-market chart data, and communities paying protection because no other force reaches them. Direct assault can scatter raiders without replacing the services they monopolize.
locations.everydayLife: Markets open only when lookout buoys signal clear approaches. Protection fees are recorded as repair subscriptions, and several captains maintain genuine rescue crews alongside coercive boarding parties.

locations.title: Shepherd Node Seven
locations.playerSummary: A regular asteroid formation suspected to contain a buried gravitic installation.
locations.directorSummary: Node Seven is one of thirteen ancient regulators. It can stabilize nearby routes, but any operational controller can turn it into a chokepoint. Access requires evidence from independent sources or a risky blind survey.
locations.everydayLife: There is no current settlement. Maintenance chambers contain dormant repair drones, mineralized control conduits, and signs that prior visitors removed components without understanding them.

locations.title: Deep Tide Trench
locations.playerSummary: The most unstable mapped region of the Reef, where transit lanes compress, reverse, or disappear.
locations.directorSummary: Deep Tide is the likely convergence zone for the final inversion but remains accessible earlier for proportionate high-risk operations. It should offer exceptional leverage, not arbitrary punishment.
locations.everydayLife: No permanent settlement survives here. Short-lived camps and sensor buoys appear after calm intervals and are abandoned when the geometry begins to sing through hull plating.

locations.title: Bellweather Anchorage
locations.playerSummary: A moving congregation of salvage tugs, clinic barges, and trading craft that relocates as routes change.
locations.directorSummary: Bellweather is the Breakwater Guild’s practical capital. Its mobility makes it resilient and difficult to regulate. It is the best source of fabrication, towing, and informal news.
locations.everydayLife: Ships dock in rotating clusters around a heavily modified tender. Disputes are settled at public tool benches where each party must contribute labor while arguing its case.

locations.title: Ossuary Loop
locations.playerSummary: A slow orbit of exodus wrecks, memorial capsules, and disputed remains near the outer edge of Saint Caligo.
locations.directorSummary: Ossuary Loop is where technical identification and cultural ownership diverge. It supports Twelve Names, A Ship Named Twice, and evidence concerning the old civilian exodus.
locations.everydayLife: Visitors approach under low power, transmit identity declarations, and attach small memory lights to designated spars. Salvage is taboo unless a memorial council grants permission.

locations.title: Lantern Span
locations.playerSummary: A promising corridor between Quiet Shelf and Aster Basin where a buoy chain could create the Reef’s first broadly usable public route.
locations.directorSummary: Lantern Span makes chart policy tangible. Public beacons improve rescue and commerce; encrypted or locally keyed beacons preserve control; central control creates dependence.
locations.everydayLife: When open, civilian pilots leave route notes and spare parts at each beacon. When closed, the span becomes an empty stretch of static and abandoned marker lights.

locations.title: Maintenance Habitat Kappa-4
locations.playerSummary: A concealed pre-Federation maintenance habitat whose location is not known at campaign start.
locations.directorSummary: Kappa-4 shelters Captain Rhos, navigator Ensign Eshren Kor, and civilian pilot Mavek Sorn after the opening disaster. Their condition depends on the Rhos Survival clock. The habitat also contains partial Shepherd maintenance records.
locations.everydayLife: The habitat is mostly dormant. Emergency lighting, fungal air scrubbers, and ancient gravity baffles keep a few chambers habitable while systems fail in uneven cycles.

factions.title: Starfleet Survey Command
factions.playerSummary: The Starfleet authority responsible for route safety, frontier surveys, and the Glass Harbor’s mission.
factions.directorSummary: Survey Command wants reliable charts, recovered personnel, and a defensible frontier picture. It is not unified about publication speed or local sovereignty.
goals: Recover Captain Rhos and the shuttle crew

factions.title: Drift Concord
factions.playerSummary: A mutual-aid network of refugee habitats, independent settlements, and civilian pilots inside the Reef.
factions.directorSummary: The Concord values sanctuary and reciprocal rescue over external jurisdiction. It can become a legitimate regional government without needing to imitate the Federation.

factions.title: Breakwater Guild
factions.playerSummary: Salvage captains, tug crews, and wreck divers who maintain much of the Reef’s practical infrastructure.
factions.directorSummary: Breakwater combines public service, family enterprise, and opportunistic salvage. It has recovered Shepherd components without understanding their system-level effects.
goals: Keep the Reef navigable enough for trade
Avoid external seizure of recovered material

factions.title: Kheled Protectorate
factions.playerSummary: A neighboring state claiming a security interest in the Reef and demanding a formal navigation regime.
factions.directorSummary: The Protectorate genuinely fears an ungoverned raider sanctuary. Tal Oren seeks negotiated control; Jorad Pel leaks route data to create the failures that justify intervention.

factions.title: Crown of Embers
factions.playerSummary: A loose confederation of raider captains, protection crews, smugglers, and isolated communities.
factions.directorSummary: The Crown is not uniform. Veyra Ninth can bargain, coerce, or provide rescue. Its consolidation depends on whether lawful actors replace the services it monopolizes.

factions.title: Shepherd Network
factions.playerSummary: No recognized political faction; evidence suggests the Reef contains linked ancient machinery.
factions.directorSummary: The network is a non-sentient but adaptive control system built to distribute gravitic stress. It resists unsafe single-point commands and can misclassify modern high-energy operations as damage.

actors.name: Commodore Lysa Mbeki
actors.title: Commander, Starfleet Survey Group Nine
actors.playerSummary: The player’s operational superior, responsible for the frontier survey group outside the Reef.
actors.directorSummary: Mbeki supports the acting command but faces political pressure for publishable charts. She will authorize discretion when given a lawful rationale and an alternative safety plan.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows Rhos’s filed route plan
Knows Starfleet survey doctrine
Does not know Kappa-4 or the complete Shepherd network

actors.name: Warden Lira Quell
actors.title: Coordinator of the Drift Concord
actors.playerSummary: The elected coordinator of Aster Basin and the Drift Concord.
actors.directorSummary: Quell is sharp, patient, and unwilling to exchange sanctuary coordinates for vague assurances. She knows Rhos attempted contact and withheld a transmission, but not where the shuttle went.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows Drift route customs
Knows Rhos sent a low-power contact request
Does not know Rhos’s current location or all Shepherd nodes

actors.name: Master Salvager Bram Ochoa
actors.title: Speaker for the Breakwater Guild
actors.playerSummary: A salvage captain whose tugs and divers keep the Reef functioning.
actors.directorSummary: Ochoa is proud of genuine rescues and defensive of dangerous recoveries. He knows a coupler taken from the overloaded Shepherd node caused unusual surges but has not connected it to the network.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows Breakwater recoveries
Knows Bellweather routes
Does not know the complete function of Shepherd components

actors.name: Prefect Tal Oren
actors.title: Commander of Kheled Watch
actors.playerSummary: The Kheled commander responsible for border security and navigation enforcement.
actors.directorSummary: Oren believes ungoverned sanctuary will become a permanent raider state. He is not responsible for the route leaks and may become a credible partner if shown evidence against Pel.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows Kheled technical archive fragments
Knows official patrol routes
Does not initially know Pel’s leaks or Rhos’s location

actors.name: Captain Veyra Ninth
actors.title: First Voice of the Crown of Embers
actors.playerSummary: The most visible captain of the Crown of Embers, equally capable of bargaining, coercion, and selective rescue.
actors.directorSummary: Veyra possesses a partial node-control key acquired through Breakwater intermediaries. She wants legitimacy if it preserves leverage and may split from more violent captains.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows Crown routes and protection clients
Knows the partial control key affects gravitic fields
Does not know the complete Shepherd quorum

actors.name: Captain Amina Rhos
actors.title: Commanding Officer, U.S.S. Glass Harbor
actors.playerSummary: The missing captain of the Glass Harbor, known for complete survey records and patient command.
actors.directorSummary: Rhos survives the opening at Kappa-4 with Ensign Kor and Mavek Sorn. Her health and options depend on elapsed time. She discovers that publishing her data would expose Aster Basin and begins reconsidering her doctrine.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows Node Seven is active
Knows the route from the opening inversion to Kappa-4
Does not know all thirteen nodes or current faction activity

actors.name: Pilot Senn Arv
actors.title: Senior Routekeeper of Aster Basin
actors.playerSummary: A veteran civilian pilot who navigates through embodied timing, hull vibration, and communal route memory.
actors.directorSummary: Arv can teach or certify route practices but rejects the claim that instrumentation is the only reproducible knowledge. Their cooperation is essential to No Fixed Stars and several conditional routes.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows local route rhythms
Knows a hidden approach to Aster
Does not know technical Shepherd architecture

actors.name: Subprefect Jorad Pel
actors.title: Kheled Intelligence and Patrol Liaison
actors.playerSummary: The Kheled officer coordinating patrol intelligence and merchant inspections.
actors.directorSummary: Pel secretly traded limited route data to Crown captains and arranged exploitable inspection failures, believing a controlled crisis is necessary to secure intervention authority.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows leaked routes and Crown contacts
Knows Oren’s intervention thresholds
Does not know the full Shepherd network or Rhos’s survival

actors.name: Archivist Nema Voss
actors.title: Keeper of the Ossuary Loop Registry
actors.playerSummary: The independent archivist who maintains memorial and ownership records for the old exodus wrecks.
actors.directorSummary: Voss knows passenger records were altered during the exodus and can identify an old maintenance route near Kappa-4. They will not release records without credible custody terms.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows exodus records and memorial claims
Knows one historical route to Kappa-4
Does not know Rhos is there at campaign start

actors.name: Doctor Aven Ro
actors.title: Medical Coordinator, Aster Basin
actors.playerSummary: The physician coordinating dispersed clinics and evacuation triage across Aster Basin.
actors.directorSummary: Ro can provide a medical relay, civilian casualty data, and care for recovered survivors. They oppose using patient manifests to locate concealed settlements.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows civilian strain and clinic capacity
Knows medical traffic routes
Does not know faction intelligence beyond patient disclosures

fronts.title: Reef Instability
fronts.summary: Gravitic stress, damaged nodes, heavy power use, and elapsed time increase the frequency and severity of route inversions.
goals: Force a regional inversion if uncorrected
stageSummaries: The Reef is unsettled but normal local practices remain effective.
Fresh soundings expire quickly and minor shear events are common.
Conditional routes fail without pilots or buoy support.
Major basins experience station-keeping disruptions.
Multiple routes invert and emergency traffic begins to accumulate.
The Deep Tide convergence is imminent.
A regional inversion is underway.

fronts.title: Civilian Strain
fronts.summary: Shortages, medical pressure, habitat failures, and displacement accumulate as routes close or requests go unanswered.
stageSummaries: Civilian systems are strained but stable.
Maintenance queues and medical delays are visible.
Several habitats require priority support.
Aster Basin and outer settlements are rationing.
Evacuation demand exceeds available capacity.
A humanitarian cascade is underway.

fronts.title: Kheled Intervention
fronts.summary: The Protectorate gathers the legal, political, and tactical basis for entering the Reef in force.
stageSummaries: Kheled Watch maintains normal border posture.
Additional inspectors and patrol craft arrive.
The Protectorate demands formal escort authority.
A task group assembles near the Gate.
Intervention orders are prepared.
Kheled forces enter the Reef under emergency authority.

fronts.title: Raider Consolidation
fronts.summary: The Crown of Embers converts route access, protection contracts, and recovered weapons into coherent regional power.
stageSummaries: The Crown remains a loose collection of captains.
Veyra coordinates limited patrol and trade activity.
Several settlements pay regular protection.
The Crown controls important routes and repair capacity.
Rival captains accept a common command structure.
The Crown functions as the Reef’s coercive government.

fronts.title: Shepherd Cascade
fronts.summary: The fragmented ancient network isolates damage and shifts load toward surviving nodes, increasing the risk of a system-level failure.
goals: Reach a stable quorum or shut down
stageSummaries: The network remains below detectable coordination.
Node feedback appears as irregular route weather.
Damaged nodes transfer load to neighboring basins.
The network closes corridors around suspected damage.
Node Seven begins autonomous stabilization attempts.
The network is approaching a forced reset.
A full cascade is active.

clocks.title: Rhos Survival and Recovery
clocks.summary: Elapsed time, injury, habitat failures, and rescue support determine the condition of Rhos, Kor, and Sorn.

thresholds.3: The survivors lose reliable long-range transmission.
thresholds.5: A medical emergency limits Rhos’s mobility.
thresholds.7: One survivor may die unless outside support has reached Kappa-4.
thresholds.8: The habitat fails; outcome depends on previously established escape or support routes.

clocks.title: Deep Tide Convergence
clocks.summary: Physical progression toward the regional inversion.

thresholds.8: Aster station-keeping and Caligo loops begin to fail.

clocks.title: Aster Station-Keeping
clocks.summary: The margin before Aster Basin’s tethered habitats begin uncontrolled drift.

thresholds.6: Several habitats break formation unless stabilized.

clocks.title: Kheled Incursion Authority
clocks.summary: The political and military authorization for a Protectorate task group to enter the Reef.

clocks.title: Crown Node Seizure
clocks.summary: Veyra and rival captains work toward controlling a Shepherd node with the partial key.

thresholds.3: Crown scouts appear near Node Seven.

clocks.title: Navigation Charter
clocks.summary: Progress toward a legitimate navigation and data-custody agreement.

thresholds.6: A charter is ratified or a rival regime becomes entrenched.

everydayLife: Pilots trade route weather as favors, subscriptions, sealed family records, and public-service bulletins.
Many habitats move periodically, so addresses are schedules and signal patterns rather than fixed coordinates.
Salvage work is a livelihood, a memorial practice, and a source of strategic danger.
Children in larger settlements learn emergency pressure discipline and route silence before advanced astronomy.
Kheled merchants and Drift communities conduct ordinary trade even while their governments dispute navigation rights.
Starfleet uniforms signify rescue to some residents and the arrival of outside chart control to others.

campaign.title: Drowned Constellation
campaign.highConcept:
Captain Amina Rhos vanished inside a gravitic inversion while the Nerine Reef was already learning to fear its own maps. Safe routes collapse without warning. Survey buoys return impossible readings. Every chart the Glass Harbor publishes can rescue a convoy, expose a hidden sanctuary, or hand a border to whoever has guns enough to enforce it.

Into that unstable frontier comes the U.S.S. Glass Harbor: small for the size of the crisis, built for survey and escort, and suddenly commanded by an XO the crew has barely learned to trust. As acting captain, you inherit Rhos's unfinished question: who gets to decide which routes become public, which communities remain hidden, and which lives are risked when the map changes under your ship?

The Reef does not forgive clean answers. Kheled patrols, drifting habitats, raider crowns, and frightened merchants all need something from Starfleet before they will tell the truth. To keep the constellation from drowning, you will need to command the ship, protect the lost, and decide whether a safe chart is still worth drawing when every line can become a weapon.
campaign.thesis: A map can save lives, expose sanctuaries, create borders, and become a weapon.
startingDirectives: Stabilize navigation and protect lives in the Nerine Reef.
Recover Captain Rhos if possible without treating civilians or hidden communities as expendable.
Preserve lawful chart custody and avoid exposing sanctuary routes without consent.

conditions.title: The Map Remembers Resolved

finalCampaignBandRules.summary: The accepted record is Drowned Constellation: multiple routes, nodes, or habitats are lost beyond ordinary recovery.
The accepted record is A Crown in the Deep: lives may survive, but coercive route power defines the new order.
The accepted record is Charted and Conquered: navigation is stabilized, but sovereignty and consent are badly compromised.
The accepted record is Sanctuary of Shadows: concealed communities survive, but a durable shared navigation regime is not achieved.
The accepted record is Open Sea: the Reef remains broadly navigable, accountable, and not dominated by one armed authority.
The Deep Tide is contained and the final record can support a defensible peace, even with unresolved costs.
The campaign reaches its authored epilogue and closes on the accepted Reef record.

pressures.summary: The civilian convoy will enter lethal shear before a prolonged shuttle search can be completed.
The Glass Harbor’s replacement gravimetric pallet produces contradictory readings under rapidly changing shear.
The tractor emitters can hold a corridor or stabilize a transport, but prolonged use overheats the port power trunks.
The senior staff has established routines under Rhos and is now evaluating the player’s judgment under genuine uncertainty.

revelations.summary: The Glass Harbor can save the convoy, preserve the ship, and conduct a limited search, but cannot maximize all three without cost.
The lane inversion is a real regional phenomenon, not sabotage or a Crown attack.
Rhos’s shuttle transmitted a low-power course correction toward the Reef interior before contact failed.
The acting command is legally temporary but operationally complete until Rhos returns or Starfleet installs a replacement.

approaches: Prioritize convoy stabilization and delegate the shuttle search to probes or a local craft.
Commit the Glass Harbor to a short, high-risk search before withdrawing to complete the rescue.
Use tractor control, transporter relays, shuttlecraft, or coordinated civilian maneuvers in any workable combination.
Request Kheled or civilian assistance and accept the political obligations that follow.
Refuse immediate acting command and require Starfleet to clarify legal authority while the crew continues emergency operations.

outcomes.summary: Most civilians survive, the ship reaches anchorage with manageable damage, and a disciplined search record preserves multiple routes to Rhos.
The search gains strong evidence or narrows Rhos’s path, but the convoy suffers avoidable losses or dispersal.

effects.summary: Rhos’s shuttle corrected toward an unknown stable volume inside the Reef.

outcomes.summary: The convoy is saved decisively, but the missing shuttle trail degrades and the crew must accept a slower search.
The ship remains in the inversion too long, saving some lives and data at the price of damage, fatigue, and a harder opening position.

delegation.risk: Delegation can preserve capacity but gives the assigned officer independent judgment.

playerConstraints: No single failed check ends the assignment.
Refusal, postponement, withdrawal, and abandonment remain consequential command choices.
Information moves only through causally justified routes.

templates.summary: Recover a prototype gravitic buoy trapped in a recurring shear pocket or decide whether the time, crew exposure, and engineering effort are worth one additional survey asset.

templates.title: Rhos’s Empty Chair
templates.summary: Crew habits, reports, and relationships continue to reference Captain Rhos while the player exercises real acting command.

surfacing.storyQuestion: How does the crew distinguish loyalty to Rhos from confidence in the player?

templates.title: The Replacement Pallet
templates.summary: The gravimetric sensor pallet drifts under rapid shear, forcing recurring choices about calibration, cross-checking, and candor in reports.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Does the command culture reward honest uncertainty or polished confidence?
surfacing.naturalTrigger: A scan contradiction, missed route, calibration stop, or disputed sounding.

templates.title: Port-Side Margin

templates.title: Thirty-Two Buoys
templates.summary: Survey buoys are finite, recoverable, tradeable, repairable, and politically consequential whenever they become public infrastructure.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Which locations deserve scarce persistent navigation support?

templates.title: Instrument and Memory
templates.summary: Ishan Tal’s trust in formal cartography repeatedly meets local knowledge that is effective, embodied, and hard to standardize.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Can Tal remain rigorous without treating instrumentation as the only valid evidence?

beatFamilies.prompt: A local pilot contradicts a scan or a chart is technically correct but operationally unsafe.

templates.title: The Shuttle She Did Not Fly

surfacing.storyQuestion: Can T’Kessa separate professional responsibility from impossible counterfactual control?

beatFamilies.prompt: A missing-person search, pilot certification, dangerous route, or direct discussion of the opening shuttle.

templates.title: The Weapon in the Chart
templates.summary: Shara zh’Thenn treats route data as tactical access and presses for security controls even when civilians need open movement.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Can security policy protect people without converting every route into classified territory?

beatFamilies.prompt: A proposed publication, leak, inspection, or hostile use of a known route.

templates.title: Patients Are Not Access Keys
templates.summary: Olan Rix resists using refugees, medical evacuation, or treatment access as leverage for intelligence and political entry.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Where does medical neutrality end when treatment and access change the political map?

beatFamilies.prompt: A refugee inspection, quarantine, medical convoy, or proposed exchange of aid for coordinates.

templates.title: What Fear Does Not Prove
templates.summary: Sorren Vale reads social pressure well but may mistake emotional certainty for factual knowledge.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Can Vale distinguish useful empathy from unsupported motive attribution?

beatFamilies.prompt: A tense negotiation, interrogation, disputed intent, or later contradiction.

templates.title: Deck Crews Are Not a Margin
templates.summary: Probe recovery, EVA, shuttle repair, and repeated emergency launches accumulate fatigue that elegant command plans can overlook.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Does command treat labor and recovery time as real operational resources?
surfacing.naturalTrigger: Repeated buoy operations, salvage recovery, shuttle deployment, or a compressed turnaround order.

beatFamilies.prompt: Repeated buoy operations, salvage recovery, shuttle deployment, or a compressed turnaround order.

templates.title: Complete Records
templates.summary: Rhos’s doctrine favors complete survey publication, but her experience at Kappa-4 forces a reassessment that may align with or challenge the player.

surfacing.storyQuestion: What does professional transparency require when accurate data can expose sanctuary?

beatFamilies.prompt: A recovered Rhos log, direct reunion, chart policy dispute, or epilogue report.

templates.title: The Senior Navigator Missing
templates.summary: With Ensign Eshren Kor missing beside Rhos, junior navigation staff must assume work they expected to perform under supervision.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Can junior officers grow without command using them as disposable replacements?

beatFamilies.prompt: A difficult sounding, delegated transit, training scene, or error by a junior officer.

templates.title: A Local Obligation
templates.summary: A grounded civilian repair, transport, memorial, or access problem may persist and become a side assignment if command action is genuinely required.

surfacing.storyQuestion: Does the Glass Harbor have a legitimate and useful role in this specific local problem?
surfacing.naturalTrigger: The ship remains nearby, capacity exists, and the concern has appeared through committed play.

beatFamilies.prompt: The ship remains nearby, capacity exists, and the concern has appeared through committed play.

templates.title: Ordinary Continuity
templates.summary: A grounded meal, ritual, hobby, celebration, or domestic routine may recur without becoming a formal quest.

cards.title: Campaign Promise
cards.content: The player is the newly promoted Commander/XO and Acting Captain of the U.S.S. Glass Harbor after Captain Amina Rhos disappears. The Nerine Reef is a persistent open world where route knowledge changes rescue, commerce, sanctuary, and control.
cards.text: The player is the newly promoted Commander/XO and Acting Captain of the U.S.S. Glass Harbor after Captain Amina Rhos disappears. The Nerine Reef is a persistent open world where route knowledge changes rescue, commerce, sanctuary, and control.

cards.title: Campaign Thesis
cards.content: A map can save lives, expose sanctuaries, create borders, and become a weapon. Present chart decisions as operational and political choices rather than abstract data management.
cards.text: A map can save lives, expose sanctuaries, create borders, and become a weapon. Present chart decisions as operational and political choices rather than abstract data management.

cards.title: Player Agency
cards.content: Never write the player character’s thoughts, dialogue, feelings, or decisions. Provide routine Starfleet competence and clear command context, then stop at the point of judgment.
cards.text: Never write the player character’s thoughts, dialogue, feelings, or decisions. Provide routine Starfleet competence and clear command context, then stop at the point of judgment.

cards.title: Acting Captain Authority
cards.content: After the prelude, the player has full operational command. Rhos remains legal captain while missing, but her possible return never automatically removes the player as principal mission commander.
cards.text: After the prelude, the player has full operational command. Rhos remains legal captain while missing, but her possible return never automatically removes the player as principal mission commander.

cards.title: Focused Staff Disagreement
cards.content: Use one or two relevant officers for disagreement. Do not turn every briefing into a full-crew moral debate or a vote on command.
cards.text: Use one or two relevant officers for disagreement. Do not turn every briefing into a full-crew moral debate or a vote on command.

cards.title: Failure Forward
cards.content: A failed check creates damage, delay, exposure, fatigue, lost leverage, an injured NPC, a harder route, or transformed objectives. It never deletes the only clue or ends the campaign.
cards.text: A failed check creates damage, delay, exposure, fatigue, lost leverage, an injured NPC, a harder route, or transformed objectives. It never deletes the only clue or ends the campaign.

cards.title: Open-World Causality
cards.content: Multiple quests may coexist; only one activity is foregrounded. Factions and physical fronts act between scenes based on goals, information, and elapsed time rather than reacting solely to the player.
cards.text: Multiple quests may coexist; only one activity is foregrounded. Factions and physical fronts act between scenes based on goals, information, and elapsed time rather than reacting solely to the player.

cards.title: U.S.S. Glass Harbor
cards.content: Steamrunner-class frontier survey and route-security vessel. Strong at sensing, escort, towing, probe work, rescue transport, and limited combat; unable to dominate the entire Reef or solve every crisis alone.
cards.text: Steamrunner-class frontier survey and route-security vessel. Strong at sensing, escort, towing, probe work, rescue transport, and limited combat; unable to dominate the entire Reef or solve every crisis alone.

cards.title: Glass Harbor Constraints
cards.content: The gravimetric pallet drifts under rapid shear; prolonged tractor use overheats port power trunks; only thirty-two dedicated gravitic buoys are carried at start. Make these constraints visible through reports and consequences, not arbitrary failure.
cards.text: The gravimetric pallet drifts under rapid shear; prolonged tractor use overheats port power trunks; only thirty-two dedicated gravitic buoys are carried at start. Make these constraints visible through reports and consequences, not arbitrary failure.

cards.title: Campaign Hidden Truth
cards.content: The Reef contains thirteen Shepherd nodes: seven functional, three damaged, two inert, and one overloaded by modern salvage. Rhos survives at Kappa-4 with Ensign Kor and Mavek Sorn. Pel leaked routes to Crown contacts. No actor knows the complete truth at start.

cards.title: Rhos Knowledge Boundary
cards.content: Rhos knows Node Seven is active, the opening route to Kappa-4, and that full publication exposes Aster. She does not know every node, Pel’s route leaks, or the current faction balance.
cards.text: Rhos knows Node Seven is active, the opening route to Kappa-4, and that full publication exposes Aster. She does not know every node, Pel’s route leaks, or the current faction balance.

cards.title: Calm Content
cards.content: The Quiet School, memorial work, calibration, meals, and ordinary repairs may remain entirely unrelated to the Shepherd network. Do not convert every local problem into central conspiracy evidence.

cards.title: Amina Rhos
cards.content: Captain Amina Rhos, Commanding Officer. Patient, exacting, and known for publishing complete survey records. Her disappearance creates the opening succession. Voice: Measured, quiet, and exact. She asks for assumptions before conclusions and rarely repeats an order.
cards.text: Captain Amina Rhos, Commanding Officer. Patient, exacting, and known for publishing complete survey records. Her disappearance creates the opening succession. Voice: Measured, quiet, and exact. She asks for assumptions before conclusions and rarely repeats an order.

cards.title: Amina Rhos — Director Guidance
cards.content: Pressure hooks: Transparent chart doctrine conflicts with sanctuary; Her return tests command continuity; She carries responsibility for the opening shuttle decision. Development dimensions: doctrinal-flexibility; trust-in-successor; accountability; recovery. Reveal boundary: Rhos’s private doubt about complete publication emerges only after recovery or authenticated logs.; Her location remains Director-only until supported by evidence.

cards.title: Ishan Tal
cards.content: Lieutenant Commander Ishan Tal, Chief Science Officer and Gravitic Cartographer. A precise, fast-speaking cartographer who believes incomplete charts kill people. Voice: Dense, exact, and candid. Tal distinguishes measurement from inference but can speak as though an error bar is a nuisance rather than a warning.

cards.title: Lagrange Gate
cards.content: The Federation-side entrance to the Nerine Reef, centered on a damaged navigation platform and a congregation of vessels waiting for escort. Everyday texture: Courier crews barter current soundings in station corridors; families sleep aboard ships waiting for safe passage; repair teams work continuously on the navigation platform.

cards.title: Lagrange Gate — Director Guidance
cards.content: Lagrange Gate is the safest communications point and the easiest location for outside authorities to project power. Crowding, inspection delays, and route scarcity turn routine traffic control into political pressure.
cards.text: Lagrange Gate is the safest communications point and the easiest location for outside authorities to project power. Crowding, inspection delays, and route scarcity turn routine traffic control into political pressure.

cards.title: Kheled Watch
cards.content: A compact Kheled patrol station controlling the Protectorate’s most reliable approach to the Reef. Everyday texture: The station is austere and heavily scheduled. Registered merchants receive predictable service; unregistered habitats and improvised craft experience delays and scrutiny.
cards.text: A compact Kheled patrol station controlling the Protectorate’s most reliable approach to the Reef. Everyday texture: The station is austere and heavily scheduled. Registered merchants receive predictable service; unregistered habitats and improvised craft experience delays and scrutiny.

cards.title: Kheled Watch — Director Guidance
cards.content: Kheled Watch can supply repair stores, military pilots, and disciplined patrol support, but it treats navigation access as a security concession. Prefect Tal Oren is pragmatic; Subprefect Jorad Pel is actively manufacturing evidence for intervention.

cards.title: Quiet Shelf
cards.content: A relatively stable basin containing a listening array, an agricultural habitat, and enough clear space for sensor calibration and flight training. Everyday texture: The agricultural habitat runs on communal shifts and treats visiting crews to fresh produce. The listening array’s technicians keep meticulous handwritten route weather logs.

cards.title: Quiet Shelf — Director Guidance
cards.content: Quiet Shelf supports calm content, repairs, crew conversations, and calibration. Time spent here is safe but allows other fronts to advance. It is not secretly a villain base.

cards.title: Aster Basin
cards.content: The largest civilian concentration in the Reef and the informal center of the Drift Concord, assembled from habitats, converted freighters, and tethered platforms around a dim brown dwarf. Everyday texture: Markets move between docked hulls, classrooms rotate through ships with available pressure, and public meetings are held by narrowband relay so no single habitat becomes a permanent capital.

cards.title: Aster Basin — Director Guidance
cards.content: Aster Basin survives through concealment, shared pilots, and mutual aid. Recording or transmitting its exact coordinates changes Chart Exposure. The basin can become a trusted ally, a resentful neutral, an occupied dependency, or a mass-casualty site.

cards.title: Saint Caligo Wreckfield
cards.content: Hundreds of wrecks from several conflicts orbit a compact gravitic knot, many stripped, some armed, and a few still sealed. Everyday texture: Breakwater divers mark safe hulls with amber lamps; memorial transmitters recite names on old emergency bands; salvage families live aboard tugs that rarely leave the field.

cards.title: Saint Caligo Wreckfield — Director Guidance
cards.content: Saint Caligo supports repeated visits. It contains survivor recoveries, cultural claims, weapons hazards, Shepherd components, and evidence from the Kheled-Crown border crisis. No single expedition exhausts the location.

cards.title: Crown Shoal
cards.content: A maze of dense fragments and sensor shadows intermittently controlled by the Crown of Embers. Everyday texture: Markets open only when lookout buoys signal clear approaches. Protection fees are recorded as repair subscriptions, and several captains maintain genuine rescue crews alongside coercive boarding parties.

cards.title: Crown Shoal — Director Guidance
cards.content: Crown Shoal contains illicit docks, hostage exchanges, black-market chart data, and communities paying protection because no other force reaches them. Direct assault can scatter raiders without replacing the services they monopolize.
cards.text: Crown Shoal contains illicit docks, hostage exchanges, black-market chart data, and communities paying protection because no other force reaches them. Direct assault can scatter raiders without replacing the services they monopolize.

cards.title: Shepherd Node Seven
cards.content: A regular asteroid formation suspected to contain a buried gravitic installation. Everyday texture: There is no current settlement. Maintenance chambers contain dormant repair drones, mineralized control conduits, and signs that prior visitors removed components without understanding them.
cards.text: A regular asteroid formation suspected to contain a buried gravitic installation. Everyday texture: There is no current settlement. Maintenance chambers contain dormant repair drones, mineralized control conduits, and signs that prior visitors removed components without understanding them.

cards.title: Shepherd Node Seven — Director Guidance
cards.content: Node Seven is one of thirteen ancient regulators. It can stabilize nearby routes, but any operational controller can turn it into a chokepoint. Access requires evidence from independent sources or a risky blind survey.
cards.text: Node Seven is one of thirteen ancient regulators. It can stabilize nearby routes, but any operational controller can turn it into a chokepoint. Access requires evidence from independent sources or a risky blind survey.

cards.title: Deep Tide Trench
cards.content: The most unstable mapped region of the Reef, where transit lanes compress, reverse, or disappear. Everyday texture: No permanent settlement survives here. Short-lived camps and sensor buoys appear after calm intervals and are abandoned when the geometry begins to sing through hull plating.

Short-lived camps and sensor buoys appear after calm intervals and are abandoned when the geometry begins to sing through hull plating.

cards.title: Deep Tide Trench — Director Guidance
cards.content: Deep Tide is the likely convergence zone for the final inversion but remains accessible earlier for proportionate high-risk operations. It should offer exceptional leverage, not arbitrary punishment.
cards.text: Deep Tide is the likely convergence zone for the final inversion but remains accessible earlier for proportionate high-risk operations. It should offer exceptional leverage, not arbitrary punishment.

cards.title: Bellweather Anchorage
cards.content: A moving congregation of salvage tugs, clinic barges, and trading craft that relocates as routes change. Everyday texture: Ships dock in rotating clusters around a heavily modified tender. Disputes are settled at public tool benches where each party must contribute labor while arguing its case.

cards.title: Bellweather Anchorage — Director Guidance
cards.content: Bellweather is the Breakwater Guild’s practical capital. Its mobility makes it resilient and difficult to regulate. It is the best source of fabrication, towing, and informal news.
cards.text: Bellweather is the Breakwater Guild’s practical capital. Its mobility makes it resilient and difficult to regulate. It is the best source of fabrication, towing, and informal news.

cards.title: Ossuary Loop
cards.content: A slow orbit of exodus wrecks, memorial capsules, and disputed remains near the outer edge of Saint Caligo. Everyday texture: Visitors approach under low power, transmit identity declarations, and attach small memory lights to designated spars. Salvage is taboo unless a memorial council grants permission.

cards.title: Ossuary Loop — Director Guidance
cards.content: Ossuary Loop is where technical identification and cultural ownership diverge. It supports Twelve Names, A Ship Named Twice, and evidence concerning the old civilian exodus.
cards.text: Ossuary Loop is where technical identification and cultural ownership diverge. It supports Twelve Names, A Ship Named Twice, and evidence concerning the old civilian exodus.

cards.title: Lantern Span
cards.content: A promising corridor between Quiet Shelf and Aster Basin where a buoy chain could create the Reef’s first broadly usable public route. Everyday texture: When open, civilian pilots leave route notes and spare parts at each beacon. When closed, the span becomes an empty stretch of static and abandoned marker lights.

cards.title: Lantern Span — Director Guidance
cards.content: Lantern Span makes chart policy tangible. Public beacons improve rescue and commerce; encrypted or locally keyed beacons preserve control; central control creates dependence.
cards.text: Lantern Span makes chart policy tangible. Public beacons improve rescue and commerce; encrypted or locally keyed beacons preserve control; central control creates dependence.

cards.title: Maintenance Habitat Kappa-4
cards.content: A concealed pre-Federation maintenance habitat whose location is not known at campaign start. Everyday texture: The habitat is mostly dormant. Emergency lighting, fungal air scrubbers, and ancient gravity baffles keep a few chambers habitable while systems fail in uneven cycles.

cards.title: Maintenance Habitat Kappa-4 — Director Guidance
cards.content: Kappa-4 shelters Captain Rhos, navigator Ensign Eshren Kor, and civilian pilot Mavek Sorn after the opening disaster. Their condition depends on the Rhos Survival clock. The habitat also contains partial Shepherd maintenance records.

cards.title: Starfleet Survey Command
cards.content: The Starfleet authority responsible for route safety, frontier surveys, and the Glass Harbor’s mission.
cards.text: The Starfleet authority responsible for route safety, frontier surveys, and the Glass Harbor’s mission.

cards.title: Starfleet Survey Command — Director Guidance
cards.content: Survey Command wants reliable charts, recovered personnel, and a defensible frontier picture. It is not unified about publication speed or local sovereignty. Goals: Recover Captain Rhos and the shuttle crew; Produce reliable navigation data; Prevent a border crisis; Preserve Starfleet credibility. Methods: Formal orders; Survey standards; Logistics support; Command review.

cards.title: Drift Concord
cards.content: A mutual-aid network of refugee habitats, independent settlements, and civilian pilots inside the Reef.
cards.text: A mutual-aid network of refugee habitats, independent settlements, and civilian pilots inside the Reef.

cards.title: Drift Concord — Director Guidance
cards.content: The Concord values sanctuary and reciprocal rescue over external jurisdiction. It can become a legitimate regional government without needing to imitate the Federation. Goals: Protect concealed communities; Keep routes locally usable; Prevent annexation; Maintain mutual aid. Methods: Pilot networks; Distributed councils; Selective disclosure; Civilian rescue.

cards.title: Breakwater Guild
cards.content: Salvage captains, tug crews, and wreck divers who maintain much of the Reef’s practical infrastructure.
cards.text: Salvage captains, tug crews, and wreck divers who maintain much of the Reef’s practical infrastructure.

cards.title: Breakwater Guild — Director Guidance
cards.content: Breakwater combines public service, family enterprise, and opportunistic salvage. It has recovered Shepherd components without understanding their system-level effects. Goals: Protect salvage livelihoods; Keep the Reef navigable enough for trade; Avoid external seizure of recovered material; Preserve crew autonomy. Methods: Towing; Fabrication; Informal contracts; Selective records.

cards.title: Kheled Protectorate
cards.content: A neighboring state claiming a security interest in the Reef and demanding a formal navigation regime.
cards.text: A neighboring state claiming a security interest in the Reef and demanding a formal navigation regime.

cards.title: Kheled Protectorate — Director Guidance
cards.content: The Protectorate genuinely fears an ungoverned raider sanctuary. Tal Oren seeks negotiated control; Jorad Pel leaks route data to create the failures that justify intervention. Goals: Control access; Suppress raiding; Protect Kheled commerce; Expand legal jurisdiction. Methods: Inspections; Patrol escorts; Archive control; Threat of intervention.

cards.title: Crown of Embers
cards.content: A loose confederation of raider captains, protection crews, smugglers, and isolated communities.
cards.text: A loose confederation of raider captains, protection crews, smugglers, and isolated communities.

cards.title: Crown of Embers — Director Guidance
cards.content: The Crown is not uniform. Veyra Ninth can bargain, coerce, or provide rescue. Its consolidation depends on whether lawful actors replace the services it monopolizes. Goals: Control profitable routes; Acquire Shepherd leverage; Prevent outside occupation; Bind dependent settlements. Methods: Protection contracts; Boarding; Trade; Hostage exchange; Selective rescue.

cards.title: Shepherd Network
cards.content: No recognized political faction; evidence suggests the Reef contains linked ancient machinery.
cards.text: No recognized political faction; evidence suggests the Reef contains linked ancient machinery.

cards.title: Shepherd Network — Director Guidance
cards.content: The network is a non-sentient but adaptive control system built to distribute gravitic stress. It resists unsafe single-point commands and can misclassify modern high-energy operations as damage. Goals: Distribute gravitic load; Prevent cascade formation; Isolate damaged nodes; Require quorum for full control. Methods: Field modulation; Route closure; Maintenance drones; Power rejection.

cards.title: Commodore Lysa Mbeki
cards.content: Commander, Starfleet Survey Group Nine. The player’s operational superior, responsible for the frontier survey group outside the Reef.
cards.text: Commander, Starfleet Survey Group Nine. The player’s operational superior, responsible for the frontier survey group outside the Reef.

cards.title: Commodore Lysa Mbeki — Director Guidance
cards.content: Mbeki supports the acting command but faces political pressure for publishable charts. She will authorize discretion when given a lawful rationale and an alternative safety plan. Knowledge boundary: Knows Rhos’s filed route plan; Knows Starfleet survey doctrine; Does not know Kappa-4 or the complete Shepherd network.

She knows Rhos attempted contact and withheld a transmission, but not where the shuttle went. Knowledge boundary: Knows Drift route customs; Knows Rhos sent a low-power contact request; Does not know Rhos’s current location or all Shepherd nodes.

cards.content: Commander of Kheled Watch. The Kheled commander responsible for border security and navigation enforcement.

cards.content: Oren believes ungoverned sanctuary will become a permanent raider state. He is not responsible for the route leaks and may become a credible partner if shown evidence against Pel. Knowledge boundary: Knows Kheled technical archive fragments; Knows official patrol routes; Does not initially know Pel’s leaks or Rhos’s location.

She discovers that publishing her data would expose Aster Basin and begins reconsidering her doctrine. Knowledge boundary: Knows Node Seven is active; Knows the route from the opening inversion to Kappa-4; Does not know all thirteen nodes or current faction activity.

cards.title: Soundings
cards.content: Join the U.S.S. Glass Harbor as its newly promoted Executive Officer, establish a working command rhythm, and take the conn when a convoy rescue and gravitic inversion strand Captain Amina Rhos beyond contact. Dramatic question: What kind of acting captain takes command when loyalty, rescue, and incomplete information collide?

cards.title: Aster Basin
cards.content: Make first sustained contact with the Drift Concord while a failing habitat tether, undocumented refugee traffic, and the risk of exposing a hidden civilian capital compete for command attention. Dramatic question: Can Starfleet help a concealed community without turning recognition into surveillance?

cards.title: The Caligo Sounding
cards.content: Enter Saint Caligo Wreckfield to recover a trapped survey team, secure unstable ordnance, and determine why wrecks from unrelated eras have migrated into the same gravitic pattern. Dramatic question: Who owns evidence and dangerous salvage when rescue, history, and security cannot be separated?

cards.title: Terms of Passage
cards.content: Resolve a transit crisis at Lagrange Gate as Kheled escorts demand inspection rights, civilian ships refuse to surrender route data, and the Reef closes around vessels waiting for lawful passage. Dramatic question: What makes a navigation regime legitimate when every safety rule also grants political access?

cards.title: The Chartmakers
cards.content: Build or reject a durable navigation regime by deciding how public buoys, local pilots, Starfleet survey records, and foreign access will interact across the Reef. Dramatic question: Who may convert knowledge of survival into governing power?

cards.title: The Sunken Fleet
cards.content: Recover survivors, remains, weapons, and records from a migrating cluster of wrecks whose history could expose Kheled misconduct, Crown predation, and an earlier civilian exodus. Dramatic question: What does accountability require when every recovered truth is also strategic leverage?

cards.title: Cities Without Stars
cards.content: Address linked settlement crises across Aster Basin and Crown-controlled routes while determining whether the Drift Concord will become a recognized government, remain a sanctuary network, or fragment under outside protection. Dramatic question: What political authority has survival created, and who has the right to recognize or replace it?

cards.title: The Shepherds Below
cards.content: Enter and repair, disable, or redistribute control of Shepherd Node Seven while rival factions seek the first strategic command point over the Reef’s gravitic geometry. Dramatic question: Can infrastructure powerful enough to govern movement be made safe without becoming an instrument of rule?

cards.title: The Deep Tide
cards.content: Command a multi-front regional inversion in which routes collapse, Aster Basin loses station-keeping, fleets enter concealed space, wrecks migrate, and every earned alliance or technical asset must be assigned under changing geometry. Dramatic question: Can the Reef survive stabilization without surrendering its future to the faction best positioned to command the crisis?

cards.title: What the Map Remembers
cards.content: Resolve the public record, navigation governance, salvage law, hidden-community status, Captain Rhos’s future, and the player’s command standing after the Deep Tide. Dramatic question: What must be remembered, withheld, repaired, or relinquished after survival?

cards.title: Bell in the Dark
cards.content: Recover and reposition a civilian rescue beacon that has become a trusted meeting point, knowing that moving it improves one route while breaking established routines elsewhere. Dramatic question: When does safer infrastructure become the loss of a community’s own geography?

cards.title: A Ship Named Twice
cards.content: Mediate between two communities that claim the same wreck as their founding vessel, where technical identification cannot settle cultural ownership. Dramatic question: Who owns a vessel after memory has made it the beginning of two peoples?

cards.title: The Quiet School
cards.content: Repair a habitat school’s atmosphere and establish a safer shuttle route without converting an ordinary community need into a strategic incident. Dramatic question: What does useful Starfleet presence look like when no conspiracy is involved?

cards.content: Support T’Kessa in certifying local pilots whose methods rely on embodied route memory, hull vibration, and communal practice rather than standard Starfleet instrumentation. Dramatic question: What counts as reproducible expertise when the environment defeats ordinary instruments?

cards.title: The Captain’s Table
cards.content: Decide which of Captain Rhos’s rituals, standing orders, and informal practices should continue under acting command and which should change. Dramatic question: How does a temporary commander create continuity without becoming an imitation or an erasure?

cards.content: Deploy a chain of low-power navigation beacons and decide whether their data is public, encrypted, locally keyed, or controlled by Starfleet. Dramatic question: Can a route be shared without giving away everything it connects?

cards.content: Move a failing generation ship through a channel too narrow for safe standard tractor operations, requiring outside tugs, staged field work, or a dangerous engineering plan. Dramatic question: How much of the Glass Harbor may command risk to save one irreplaceable community?

cards.content: Identify remains from a wreck whose passenger list was falsified, knowing that the result will affect inheritance, asylum, criminal liability, and a public memorial. Dramatic question: What is owed to the dead when truthful identification destabilizes the living?

cards.content: Recover a prototype gravitic buoy trapped in a recurring shear pocket or decide whether the time, crew exposure, and engineering effort are worth one additional survey asset. Dramatic question: When does scarcity justify risking people to recover equipment?

cards.title: Thirteen Shepherd Nodes
cards.content: Fixed truth: thirteen nodes exist. Seven function, three are damaged, two inert, and one overloaded through salvage. The network is adaptive but not sentient and requires quorum for safe large-scale control.

cards.title: Rhos at Kappa-4
cards.content: Fixed truth: Rhos, Ensign Eshren Kor, and civilian pilot Mavek Sorn survive the opening and reach Kappa-4. Their health and available testimony depend on elapsed time and rescue conditions.

cards.title: Pel’s Controlled Crisis
cards.content: Fixed truth: Jorad Pel leaked selected route data to Crown contacts and shaped inspection failures to justify Kheled intervention. Tal Oren did not initially know.

cards.title: Crown Partial Key
cards.content: Fixed truth: Veyra Ninth possesses a partial Shepherd control key acquired through Breakwater intermediaries. It can force local changes but cannot safely command the network.

cards.title: Deep Tide Cause
cards.content: Fixed truth: the final inversion results from network-wide stress redistribution accelerated by the overloaded salvage node and modern high-energy traffic. It is not a villain’s single switch.

canonEra.dominionWarStatus: The Dominion War is underway but remains distant strategic context unless campaign events create a causal connection.
canonEra.voyagerStatus: Voyager remains in the Delta Quadrant and does not intersect the Glass Harbor campaign.
canonEra.canonBoundary: Use canon factions, technology, and era conditions as background. The ship, crew, region, local factions, and campaign outcomes are original and canon-adjacent.

missionDirectionRules: Situation on rails, solution off rails.
Every dynamic event must have a causal parent in committed state, actor goals, physical processes, or elapsed time.
The Nerine Reef remains a persistent region; locations, routes, factions, and requests continue outside the foreground quest.
Calm side assignments and ordinary crew scenes may remain unrelated to the Shepherd network.
Crew disagreement should be focused; not every senior officer gives a moral speech before action.
No character begins with the complete campaign truth.
Move information only when causally justified if the player bypasses an expected scene.
Escalation pacing serves the situation rather than forcing combat or countdowns.
Factions act between player scenes based on goals, knowledge, capabilities, and current access.
Maps and sensor information are operational objects with custody, revision history, access rules, and consequences.
hiddenInformation: Exact Shepherd node count before discovery
The Crown’s partial Shepherd control key
Predetermined valid solutions or Command Decisions
failurePolicy: No single failed check ends a mission.
Campaign-critical survival preserves meaningful continuation after major loss, refusal, delay, or withdrawal.
A failed scan creates uncertainty, false confidence, delay, equipment strain, exposure, or a harder evidence route; it does not delete the only clue.
Senior-crew death requires an established lethal situation, meaningful player choice or accumulated consequence, fair opportunity for command response, and narrative weight.
Refusing an assignment is a consequential command act, not an out-of-bounds input.
Delay advances clocks only when actors or physical processes have a reason to continue.
The Glass Harbor may be heavily damaged, forced to withdraw, or lose regional leverage without invalidating the campaign.
playerFacingInfo: Known route condition and public travel estimate
